events.text: {{characters.soren.name}} spread his hand-drawn route map among them.

events.speaker: {{characters.soren.name}}
events.text: Solmaris bases its core belief on soul purification. Members have had varying degrees of sensory modification. From the data we've obtained, their strongholds are primarily underground; almost no permanent structures above ground.

events.speaker: {{characters.soren.name}}
events.text: After contact with them, our approach is cooperation, no attempts at escape or conflict. Let them bring us in. Primary objective after entry is observation and intelligence gathering.

events.speaker: {{characters.soren.name}}
events.text: Any problems?

events.speaker: {{characters.lyra.name}}
events.text: Yes. If Solmaris people ask why we came, how do we answer?

events.speaker: {{characters.soren.name}}
events.text: The truth. We're seeking the cause behind this war's outbreak and whether a larger controlling force exists behind the three-way conflict. That's true, Solmaris should understand that motivation.

events.speaker: {{characters.elias.name}}
events.text: That's too direct. You're stating our real objective outright. If Solmaris doesn't want anyone investigating this, you've just marked us as threats.

events.speaker: {{characters.soren.name}}
events.text: Then what's your suggestion?

events.speaker: {{characters.elias.name}}
events.text: Say we're travelers just passing through.

events.speaker: {{characters.alea.name}}
events.text: That's more suspicious. There are no travelers in the wasteland, only people running and people on missions. Say just passing through and their first reaction is you're lying.

events.speaker: {{characters.elias.name}}
events.text: Then say we're fleeing. That's true—we did escape from Terminal Ark.

events.speaker: {{characters.soren.name}}
events.text: Saying we're fleeing is fine. But that doesn't explain why we deliberately approach Solmaris's patrol zone. People fleeing would avoid it, wouldn't walk there.
The three fell silent. That question hung there.

events.speaker: {{characters.lyra.name}}
events.text: I think... <delay ms="400"/>we came because we want to find a way for everyone to survive.
The three turned to look at her.

{{characters.soren.name}} watched her. {{characters.elias.name}} froze with the small stone in his hand. {{characters.alea.name}}'s gaze moved away from {{characters.lyra.name}}'s face to glance at the ground beside her.

events.speaker: {{characters.elias.name}}
events.text: That answer... <delay ms="300"/>can't be refuted.

events.speaker: {{characters.lyra.name}}
events.text: Because it's true. We definitely came because of this. No matter whether we say it out loud, we arrived here because we want to know what's happening, want to know if there's a way to make it stop.

events.speaker: {{characters.lyra.name}}
events.text: And if Solmaris people still think we're threats after hearing this, that tells us they don't want everyone to survive. We'll know what kind of organization they are. That intelligence is useful too.
{{characters.elias.name}} put down that stone and glanced at {{characters.soren.name}}.
{{characters.soren.name}} paused, picked up the charcoal stick, and in the corner of that route map, wrote that sentence down.

events.speaker: {{characters.soren.name}}
events.text: Acceptable. We'll say that.
{{characters.lyra.name}} watched him write that sentence into his notes, her face carrying an expression she herself might not have realized.

events.speaker: {{characters.alea.name}}
events.text: When do we depart?

events.speaker: {{characters.soren.name}}
events.text: Tomorrow morning. Rest tonight, restore synchronization. The road tomorrow is long.
All four began moving, organizing their belongings, preparing for the night. The wasteland wind kept blowing.
That route map still lay spread out. That sentence written in the corner remained visible in the dim light, sitting quietly there, waiting for tomorrow.
